description: Assigns values to variables using natural syntax. Supports numeric values, point tuples, vector literals, and matrix literals. Variables can reference other variables and be used in any subsequent expression.
utility: Define variables, point tuples, vectors, and matrices using natural assignment

arguments.name: variable
arguments.description: Variable name to assign to (left side)

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: Value or expression to assign (right side)

alternates.description: Assign result of expression involving other variables

examples.expression:
A = point(G, 0, 0)
B = point(G, 3, 4)
L = line(G, A, B)

A = (2, 1)
B = 1.3 * (4, 2)
graph_1 = g2d(at(2.7, 3.4), size(11.7), range(-1, 6), type(geometry))
pt = point(graph_1, B)
l1 = line(graph_1, A, B)
